name: Filter then Compare
summary: First build a cleaned copy — only letters and digits, all lowercased — then two-pointer check that copy. Simpler logic, but it uses O(n) extra memory for the filtered array.
visualizationIntent: build (L3-6): starting from an empty filtered array, scan the input and let each alphanumeric character, lowercased, pop into filtered — the extra O(n) array visibly fills. check (L7-14): two-pointer over filtered; left/right converge, compare (L10) marks the pair compared, a mismatch (L11) flashes error and returns false, matches lock visited as pointers step inward, and crossing returns true (L14). The learner sees that a whole cleaned copy had to be built.

lineExplanations.3: Create an empty array to hold the cleaned characters.
lineExplanations.4: Look at each character of the input.
lineExplanations.6: Append the lowercased character to the cleaned copy.
lineExplanations.7: Point left at the start of the cleaned array.
lineExplanations.8: Point right at the end of the cleaned array.
lineExplanations.9: Compare inward until the pointers meet.
lineExplanations.10: Compare the two ends (already lowercased, so a direct compare works).
lineExplanations.12: Matched — move the left pointer inward.
lineExplanations.14: All pairs matched — it's a palindrome.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isPalindrome(self, s): — s is the raw string.
syntaxExplanations.3: filtered = [] — an empty list for the cleaned characters (the extra memory).
syntaxExplanations.4: for ch in s: — look at each character of the input.
syntaxExplanations.5: if ch.isalnum(): — keep only letters and digits.
syntaxExplanations.6: filtered.append(ch.lower()) — add the lowercased character to the clean copy.
syntaxExplanations.7: left = 0 — pointer at the start of the cleaned array.
syntaxExplanations.8: right = len(filtered) - 1 — pointer at the end of the cleaned array.
syntaxExplanations.9: while left < right: — compare inward until the pointers meet.
syntaxExplanations.10: if filtered[left] != filtered[right]: — the copy is already lowercase, so a direct compare works.
syntaxExplanations.11: return False — a mismatch means it's not a palindrome.
syntaxExplanations.14: return True — all pairs matched — it's a palindrome.
